sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.102 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: We say that two subsets$A, B$of an infinite group$G$are separated\/ if there exists an infinite subset$X$of$G$such that$1 X$,$X=X^{-1}$, and$XAX B=$. Is it true that any two disjoint subsets$A, B$of an infinite group$G$satisfying$|A|<|G|$,$|B|<|G|$are separated? This is so if$A,B$are finite, or$G$is Abelian. { I.\,V.\,Protasov}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
